en.extract:
The streak-breasted honeyeater or streaky-breasted honeyeater (Meliphaga reticulata) is a species of bird in the family Meliphagidae. It is found on Timor island. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest, subtropical or tropical mangrove forest, and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest.

The '''streak-breasted honeyeater''' or '''streaky-breasted honeyeater''' ('''''Meliphaga reticulata''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Meliphagidae]]. It is found on [[Timor]] island.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Streak-breasted Honeyeater |url=https://ebird.org/species/stbhon3 |access-date=2023-08-19 |website=eBird |language=en}}</ref> Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]], subtropical or tropical [[mangrove forest]], and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]].
